---
title: "Voice of Monetary Policy Replication"
author: "Minna, Lasse, Alex"
format:
  revealjs:
    output-file: index.html
---


## Motivation
- US Federal Reserve (Fed) releases interest rate decisions 
- via Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings (incl. press conference)


::: {.notes}

- using high-frequency variables, we can determine quasi-causally, how big the impact of the FOMC meetings is on certain financial variables

:::


## Problem Setting

- does the inclusion of audio recording of FOMC press recording give superior information for markets?


::: {.notes}

financial variables and macro variables (= high-frequency?)

:::


## Current SOTA

- Voice of MP paper (Gorodnichenko et al. 2023)
  - Deep learning/neural network model for speech emotion detection 
  - BERT for text analysis 
- shows: positive emotion in audio of FOMC press-conference means hike in S&P500 (controlled for text sentiment)


## Our Approach

::: {.incremental}
1. Replicate Gorodnichenko et al. 2023 for ECB
2. Europe: multi-lingual currency union. How does voice tone affect markets differently?
3. Audio: Compare North American Speech vs. European Speech for training
4. Text: Compare effectiveness of different text embeddings & different sentiment analysis methods
:::

::: {.notes}
Ask the group? what do you think of the scope?
:::

## Our Approach: Audio

- *Baseline*: Replicate the original paper and train on RAVDESS + TESS.
- *European-Accent Dataset*: Use a dataset featuring European non-native English speakers (eNTERFACE'05/GEMEP/CREMA-D).
- *Multilingual Pre-Trained Model*: Fine-tune a pre-trained speech model (e.g. Wav2Vec2-XLSR, HuBERT)

## Our Approach: Text




## Evaluation Protocols
- Benchmark: North American Speech vs. European Speech 
- 


## Datasets, Data Collection
- Datasets: Transcripts and records of ECB press conferences, available on the ECB Website and YouTube
- Scraping of ECB Website + YouTube API


## Results
tbd. 


## Questions
- Supervision?
- Open for Adjustments to Approach!
- Compute?
